destinations.evidence: Model metadata is resolved by exact model name from ModelPricingRegistry contract storage; missing models return None.
LLM pricing requires the exact decoded model to exist in the on-chain registry and explicitly ignores executor-supplied metadata for pricing.
The shared fee calculation passes the database and model registry contract into the LLM pricing path and propagates missing-model errors.

equivalence_argument: Static registry and candidate-name fallback are absent from the port async-fees crate; the effective LLM path uses exact contract storage lookup and fails when the exact model name is missing.
notes: Mapped to the port model registry reader and LLM pricing resolver.
rationale: The primary commit removes the static LLM_MODEL_REGISTRY and llm_model_candidates fallback path, adds read_model_from_db(), and changes compute_llm_executor_fee()/calculate_async_fees() to require a database and model registry contract for LLM pricing. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: LLM executor fee calculation must resolve the model by exact name from the ModelPricingRegistry contract storage and must not use the removed static model registry, candidate-name normalization, executor metadata, or byte-based HTTP pricing as a fallback when the model is missing.
suggested_falsification_tests: Populate a mock ModelPricingRegistry storage with one exact model name and verify compute_llm_executor_fee succeeds only for that exact name; then call it with a case-normalized, shortened, missing, or metadata-only model and assert it returns an error rather than falling back to static defaults or HTTP byte pricing.
summary: LLM pricing uses exact on-chain model registry data and rejects missing models.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Populate only one exact model name in registry storage and assert case-normalized, shortened, missing, or metadata-only names return Err rather than fallback pricing.
title: LLM pricing uses exact on-chain model data
